nativeRange.habitat: The Caribbean Redspotted Hawkfish inhabits rocky reef areas and rubble zones throughout its range, tending to rest motionless in crevices and shallow caves. It has been recorded at depths from 2 to 46 m.

diet.notes: Primarily feeds on small crustaceans including copepods, shrimps, shrimp larvae, crabs and crab larvae, and polychaetes. In captivity has been observed accepting a variety of marine meats, frozen preparations, and live feeder shrimp.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in mouth
larger aggressive hawkfish of other species

imageCandidates.notes: NOAA Photo Library, 2592x1944. Sharp in-situ shot clearly showing red spots on head and forebody, dark vertical bars on rear, black caudal-peduncle blotch and dorsal cirri; whole fish in frame.
NOAA, St. Croix, 1194x839. Shows diagnostic red spots, dark bars and dorsal cirri while perched in a crevice; slightly softer and head partly shadowed, so kept as a non-recommended extra.

summary: A hardy, small Caribbean hawkfish that perches on rockwork and rubble. Generally considered suitable for beginners; tends to prey on small shrimp and tiny fish, so reef compatibility warrants caution with ornamental invertebrates.
careNotes: Caribbean Redspotted Hawkfish are generally considered easy to keep and tend to adapt well to aquarium life. They characteristically perch on rock surfaces, often resting motionless for extended periods — behavior consistent with their ambush-predator lifestyle documented in the wild. They have been known to prey on small ornamental shrimp and small fish, so housing with valued shrimp species is generally not advised. The species has been reported to be semi-aggressive toward conspecifics and should typically be kept singly unless a mated pair is available. Plenty of rockwork with caves and crevices is advisable to provide perching and hiding spots.
breedingNotes: No documented captive breeding reports are available in the standard hobby literature. The species is noted as a pelagic spawner in the wild.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (9.5 cm SL), native range (Western Atlantic: S. Florida, Bahamas, Caribbean, to Rio de Janeiro, Brazil), temperature range (25–28.4°C), taxonomy (Cirrhitidae / Centrarchiformes), and habitat (rocky reef/rubble, 2–46 m depth). Temperature disagreement with LiveAquaria (22.2–25.6°C vs 25–28.4°C): averaged to 23–27°C. Adult size: LiveAquaria states 3.5 in (8.9 cm), FishBase states 9.5 cm SL; upper bound of 9.5 cm used.

fish.breedingNotes: No documented captive breeding records are available in the hobby literature. The species spawns in open water (pelagic spawning) in the wild. Possible protogynous hermaphroditism — a pattern in which females can change sex to male — has been reported but has not yet been histologically confirmed.
